reasoning: The Lean statement encodes "infinitely many primes ≡ -1 (mod 4)" via the standard ∀ N, ∃ p ≥ N pattern, which is logically equivalent to the set of such primes being infinite in ℕ. The condition `p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]` unfolds to 4 ∣ (p+1), i.e., p ≡ -1 (mod 4), matching the informal congruence exactly. `Nat.Prime p` captures primality, and there are no extra or missing hypotheses. Hence the formalization is strictly faithful.
